title: Lauki Sabzi with Dal and Rice
description: A complete no-onion, no-garlic plate: soft bottle-gourd sabzi, simple dal, and rice. Everyday, light, and Jain-friendly.
explanation: This is a full lunch or dinner plate, not a side alone. Lauki keeps the meal high-volume and gentle on digestion; dal adds protein and body; rice makes it filling without heavy masala.
Flavor comes from hing, cumin, tomato, and turmeric rather than onion or garlic, so the plate stays suitable for Jain and no-onion/no-garlic households.
ingredients: 2 cups Lauki (bottle gourd), peeled and cubed
1/2 cup Yellow Moong Dal or Toor Dal, rinsed
Lemon wedges and coriander to serve
instructions: Cook rice as usual and keep warm. Boil or pressure-cook dal with water, a pinch of turmeric, and salt until soft; mash lightly and keep pourable.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add cumin and hing; when aromatic, add tomato and optional chilli. Cook until tomato softens.
Add lauki, remaining turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Stir 1–2 minutes.
Cover and cook on low–medium heat until lauki is tender, 10–12 minutes, adding a splash of water if the pan dries.
Temper the dal lightly with remaining oil, cumin, and hing if you like a separate tadka. Plate rice, dal, and lauki sabzi. Finish with lemon and coriander.
stepNotes: Choose young lauki with soft seeds; large spongy pieces cook unevenly.
Keep sabzi slightly moist rather than dry so it pairs well with rice.
Moong dal is lighter; toor dal is more traditional with rice—use what you digest well.
Skip green chilli for a milder Jain plate; black pepper works at the end instead.
Batch-cook dal and rice; make the sabzi fresh so the vegetable stays bright.

variationSections.title: Seasonal vegetable swaps
items: Use turai, tinda, or cabbage with the same hing-cumin-tomato base.
Add a handful of peas for color and sweetness.
Stir chopped spinach into the dal in the last few minutes.

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: For fewer calories, serve more sabzi and less rice.
For more protein, increase the dal portion or add a side of sprouts.
Serve with kachumber or cut fruit when you want extra produce volume.

nutrition.servingSize: 1 plate with rice and dal (half recipe)
